vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding current biological presence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/unidentifiable. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visually confirmed as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable plant occupant.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant material visible in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint foliage present.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos leaf and rabbit anchor confirmed.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No identifiable silver guest foliage present.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty soil surface; no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Contains only soil and debris; no Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: Single Pothos leaf remains; rabbit anchor is stable.
plant_audit.P4_Shared_Black_Pot: No evidence of Silver Guest; soil appears dormant.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently damp/dark across all pots; no visible surface cracking.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot (P3/P4 area) acting as a calcium amendment or soil marker.

temporal_deltas.T_earliest_to_T_minus_4: Introduction of white cable/wire structure near the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_3_to_T_minus_1: Progressive loss of light/visibility due to environmental lighting shifts; plant posture remains static.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_1_to_current: Return of visibility confirms no growth or recovery; the Pothos leaf remains in a state of stasis.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe neglect or dormancy. The Pothos (P3) is the only survivor, though it shows no signs of active growth. The other pots are effectively barren, suggesting either total plant loss or extreme subterranean dormancy.
anomalies: White cable/wire introduced near P1 (Yellow Pot) at T-4.
Persistent lack of foliage in P1, P2, and P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is currently failing to support the registered diversity. The Pothos in the black pot is the sole occupant showing signs of life, though it is not thriving. The yellow pot and the secondary black pot are devoid of visible vegetation, indicating a systemic failure of the intended biome. The introduction of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil enrichment, but the lack of response from the plants suggests the environment is currently stagnant.
